title: Read canonical producer component in Chronik history queries
goal: Correct the Chronik history component filter so producer identity is read from source.component, while subject remains reserved for the scoped work target and existing target, operation, task-class and outcome filters remain unchanged.

acceptance.assertion: query_history(component=...) matches canonical source.component and does not require or fabricate subject.component.
Repository- and host-scoped subject identity remains the work target and is not reinterpreted as producer identity.
Any compatibility behavior for historical noncanonical records is explicit, tested and cannot override a present canonical source.component.
Tests cover repository and host subjects, positive and negative producer matches, and combinations with repo, operation, task-class and outcome filters.
A deployed query including component=grabowski returns the observed HausKI completion event and preserves ledger-integrity diagnostics.
Chronik tests, local validation, GitHub CI, deploy and post-deploy query pass at the exact reviewed head.

rollback.trigger: The change confuses producer and target identity, broadens matches unexpectedly or weakens ledger validation.
rollback.procedure: Restore the previous query implementation, retain the scoped-event schema and require callers to omit the component filter until a corrected reviewed patch is deployed.

conditions: a foreign Chronik worktree, lease or process overlaps coding_memory.py or its tests
the fix would rewrite stored events instead of correcting query interpretation
source.component is absent from canonical current producer events

live_finding.root_cause_still_plausible: query_history still compares the requested producer component against subject.component instead of canonical source.component.

coordination.decision: T007 was implemented only in the isolated source-component-query-t007-v1 worktree. The canonical Chronik checkout and all foreign processes remained untouched.

does_not_establish: failure of repository, operation, task-class or outcome filters
permission to modify the active T006 worktree
need to rewrite the append-only ledger

reevaluation.priority_decision: T007 closes before any T008 work begins.
reevaluation.reason: The producer/target identity defect was reproduced, corrected at source.component, merged, deployed and confirmed by the exact HausKI query without rewriting the ledger.
reevaluation.next_safe_action: Close T007 in Bureau, then evaluate T008 separately; this closeout does not begin T008.

metadata.outcome: Chronik history queries now interpret component as the canonical producer identity event.source.component, keep subject reserved for repository or host targets, provide no legacy subject.component fallback, and return the known HausKI completion event under the deployed combined producer, target, operation, task-class and outcome filters.

deployment.import_service: inactive/dead after successful one-shot, exit status 0

validation.ruff: PASS with existing compact-test exclusions E401, E501, E701 and E702
validation.github_ci: 16 of 16 checks passed at reviewed head

acceptance_evidence.producer-field-correct: PR #223 changes the component predicate from subject.component to the single _event_source_component(event) helper reading source.component only.
acceptance_evidence.target-semantics-preserved: Repository and host target matching remains in _matches_target(subject, ...); dedicated repository and host regression tests pass.
acceptance_evidence.legacy-boundary-explicit: A missing source.component remains an invalid-record boundary and never falls back to subject.component; a present source component cannot be overridden.
acceptance_evidence.filter-regression-tests: Four new tests cover repository and host targets, positive and negative producer matches, override resistance, no fallback and conjunction with operation, task_class and outcome.
acceptance_evidence.live-readback: The deployed exact HausKI query returns only event sha256:48e2f68452689dd078397d420a9bcf55310cdda9bffbfdfafbba95c9f0342b42 with 1115 valid and 0 invalid ledger records.
acceptance_evidence.full-regression-proof: 23 focused tests, 309 full tests, 16 GitHub checks, merge-blob equality, 148 release-blob checks, versioned deploy and post-deploy query passed.

establishes: the reviewed producer component interpretation is merged and deployed
the exact observed HausKI historical event is returned by the combined deployed query
the observed ledger snapshot is valid and unchanged by the query
the Chronik import timer is active/waiting and the successful one-shot service is inactive/dead
does_not_establish: current CI state after the recorded observation

transparent_failed_invocation.reason: The final pytest command omitted PYTHONPATH=src and produced import-only collection errors; all preceding JSON, hash, check, doctor and strict registry-truth gates passed.

bureau_closeout_validation.truth_boundary: These local receipts precede the final commit and PR CI; final merge readiness still requires exact-head local revalidation and green GitHub CI.
